{"query":"2026/27 UEFA Champions League qualification second round results Larne Shamrock Rovers The New Saints","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1190,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/news/02a6-20e5a8be4e63-ae971c582f8c-1000--champions-league-qualifying-fixtures-results-dates-how-it-/","title":"Champions League qualifying: Fixtures, results, dates, how it works | UEFA Champions League | UEFA.com","content":"UEFA Champions League qualifying for the 2026/27 season starts on 7 July and concludes on 26 August.
The Champions Path contains teams that qualified as the winners of their domestic league, and the League Path contains teams that qualified through domestic leagues offering more than one place in the competition.
Qualifying for the UEFA club competitions has been specifically designed to ensure that teams from a minimum of 37 national associations make their way to the league phase of the Champions League, Europa League or Conference League.
Losing sides from the qualifying rounds of the Champions League and Europa League are given another chance to progress when they parachute down into the Europa League or Conference League.
Losing sides from the Champions League and Europa League play-offs go directly into the league phase of the Europa League and Conference League respectively.
For more information about the qualifying competition, including the rules around player registration, see the official regulations.
We explain how qualifying works and list all the fixtures and results.
First qualifying round: 7/8 & 14/15 July 2026 (Draw: 16 June)
Third qualifying round: 4/5 & 11 August 2026 (Draw: 20 July)
Champions Path: 28 (enter in this round)
Second legs: Tuesday 25/Wednesday 26 August
The league phase draw for the 2026/27 UEFA Champions League takes place on Thursday 27 August.","date":"2 days ago"},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026%E2%80%9327_UEFA_Champions_League_qualifying","title":"2026–27 UEFA Champions League qualifying - Wikipedia","content":"Of these, 42 teams are competing in the Champions Path and 10 in the League Path.
The team that scores more goals on aggregate over the two legs advances to the next round.
If the aggregate score is level at the end of normal time of the second leg, extra time will be played, and if the same number of goals is scored by both teams during extra time, the tie will be determined by a penalty shoot-out.
Schedule for 2026–27 UEFA Champions League qualifying phase and play-off round
Play-off round — 3 August 2026 — 18–19 August 2026 — 25–26 August 2026
Second qualifying round (24 teams): 10 teams which enter in this round, and 14 winners of the first qualifying round.
The 12 losers of the second qualifying round enter the Europa League Champions Path third qualifying round
Shamrock Rovers — 19.375
The default access list may be altered based on the qualifying teams' 2026 UEFA club coefficients should the winners of the 2025–26 UEFA Europa League also qualify for the 2025–26 edition via their domestic league.
Shamrock Rovers won 5–3 on aggregate.
The draw for the second qualifying round was held on 17 June 2026.
The first legs will be played on 21 and 22 July, and the second legs will be played on 28 and 29 July 2026.
Ararat-Armenia — Match 6 — Shamrock Rovers — 21 Jul — 28 Jul

A total of 52 teams are competing in the qualifying system of the 2026–27 UEFA Champions League, which includes the qualifying phase and the play-off round. Of these, 42 teams are competing in the Champions Path and 10 in the League Path. The seven winners of the play-off round—five from the Champions Path and two from the League Path—will advance to the league phase, where they will join the 29 teams that have already entered the league phase. The qualifying phase and play-off round are split into two paths – the Champions Path and the League Path.
